CREATE TABLE dates (id INTEGER, event_date DATE);
SELECT date_part('month', event_date) FROM dates WHERE id = 2;
SELECT id, date_part('day', event_date) AS day FROM dates WHERE date_part('year', event_date) = 1995;
SELECT id, date_part('month', event_date) FROM dates WHERE event_date BETWEEN DATE '1990-01-01' AND DATE '2000-01-01';
SELECT id, date_part('year', event_date) + 5 AS future_year FROM dates;
SELECT d1.id, date_part('day', d1.event_date) FROM dates d1 JOIN dates d2 ON d1.id <> d2.id WHERE date_part('year', d1.event_date) = date_part('year', d2.event_date);
SELECT d.id, date_part('year', event_date) AS year, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY date_part('year', event_date)) AS year_count FROM dates d;
SELECT d1.id FROM dates d1 WHERE date_part('year', d1.event_date) = (SELECT MAX(date_part('year', d2.event_date)) FROM dates d2);
CREATE TABLE more_dates (id INTEGER, date_check DATE);
INSERT INTO more_dates VALUES (1, DATE '2000-01-01'), (2, DATE '1998-08-08');
SELECT m.id, date_part('month', m.date_check) FROM more_dates m WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM dates d WHERE date_part('year', d.event_date) = 2000);
